author: Dr. Jessica Tanaka
content:
RHEUMATOLOGY OUTPATIENT FOLLOW-UP — 4-WEEK POST-DISCHARGE
Date/Time: 4/22/2027, 10:00 AM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Visit funded by: Hopkins financial-aid grant (one of two pre-approved visits).
Interpreter: In-person Spanish-speaking interpreter present throughout.

INTERVAL EVENTS (3/25/2027 discharge through today):

Ms. Sandoval has been discharged 4 weeks. She is brought to clinic by her mother who is also her primary caregiver. She walked into the clinic today using a cane with mother's arm support — significantly improved from her wheelchair-dependent state at discharge.

Patient self-report (interpreted, with mother nodding agreement):
- Strength: 'Mucho mejor' (much better). She can now: rise from a chair without assistance (slow but unaided); walk to the kitchen (~15 steps) with a cane; brush her own hair; lift small objects. Cannot yet climb stairs unassisted but practices with mother daily.
- Swallow: SUBSTANTIALLY IMPROVED. NG tube was REMOVED on 4/15/2027 after repeat MBSS showed minimal residual oropharyngeal dysphagia with safe handling of nectar-thick liquids, soft solids, and regular consistency under chin-tuck precautions. She has been on full PO diet since 4/15 (1 week). Tolerating soft and regular textures including her mother's cooking (caldo de pollo with shredded chicken, frijoles, tortillas torn into small pieces, atol de elote). She uses chin tuck for liquids per SLP and reports no choking events.
- Appetite: 'Tengo hambre ahora' (I am hungry now). Eating 3 meals + 2 snacks daily. Drinks Ensure as supplement.
- Weight: 44.8 kg today (up 9.4 kg from admission nadir of 35.4 kg; up 5.4 kg from discharge weight of 39.4 kg; total ~25% of way back to baseline 50.8 kg).
- Skin: 'Flaky paint' dermatosis has substantially resolved. Some residual hyperpigmentation. Hair is still thinning but mother sees new growth ('pelitos nuevos') at the scalp; 'flag sign' less prominent in newly-grown hair.
- Mood: PHQ-9 today = 6 (mild). Continues weekly therapy with Esperanza Behavioral Health (Spanish-speaking). Reports feeling 'more like myself.'
- Joint pain: 'Muy poquito' (very little). Morning stiffness minimal.
- New issues: Mild facial puffiness from steroids (anticipated). No new symptoms. No fever, no rash, no dyspnea, no chest pain, no headache, no new weakness, no choking.

MEDICATIONS (verified):
- Prednisone 30 mg PO daily (per planned taper: 40 mg x 2 weeks → 30 mg current week; next decrease to 25 mg in 1 week).
- Mycophenolate 1.5 g PO BID (escalated to goal dose 2 weeks ago without issue).
- Hydroxychloroquine 200 mg PO BID.
- Levothyroxine 50 mcg PO daily.
- Trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole 80/400 mg M/W/F (PCP prophylaxis).
- Calcium 500 mg BID + vitamin D3 2000 IU daily.
- Omeprazole 20 mg daily.
- Multivitamin daily.
- (Zinc sulfate, selenium, thiamine — completed prescribed courses.)
- Contraception: Patient and mother discussed at PCP visit and again with us today. Patient remains not sexually active and not planning conception. Pelvic exam at PCP was unremarkable. Provided with information about future fertility planning — when she is ready, she would need to transition off MMF to a pregnancy-compatible regimen (azathioprine + HCQ) at least 6 weeks before attempted conception.

LABS (drawn 4/22/2027 today, send-outs returning 4/25):
- CBC: WBC 4.40 (normal!), Hgb 10.8, Plt 232 — all in normal range. Pancytopenia resolved.
- CMP: Cr 0.5, Alb 3.4 (up from 2.3 at discharge!), Total protein 6.8 (now normal), Glu 98 (off insulin since 4/8 as prednisone tapered).
- Prealbumin: 28 (up from 13 at discharge; now within normal range). NUTRITIONAL RECOVERY EVIDENT.
- CK: 218 (continued decrease from peak 2580, now near normal).
- ESR: 22 (improved from 48); CRP: 0.4 (now normal).
- Anti-dsDNA: 16 (down from 28 at discharge); C3: 86, C4: 11 (continued improvement, near-normal).
- UPC: 0.15 (now within normal range — < 0.2; renal involvement IMPROVED, renal biopsy now not indicated).
- TSH: 2.84, free T4: 1.2 (well-controlled).
- Vitamin D: 32 (now adequate), zinc: 78 (improved), folate normal, B12 normal.

VITALS: BP 116/72, HR 76, RR 14, T 98.2 F, SpO2 99% RA. Weight 44.8 kg, BMI 19.9 (no longer underweight).

FOCUSED EXAM:
General: Marked improvement. Looks like a different person from admission photo (kept in chart). Color good, less cachetic, less drawn. Cushingoid facies mild (steroid-related).
HEENT: Oral mucosa healthy, no ulcers. Hair regrowth visible at hairline.
CV: RRR, no murmurs. JVP flat.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally.
MSK: Synovitis absent. Joints non-tender. Strength: hip flexion 4+/5, shoulder abduction 4+/5, neck flexion 4+/5, all distal 5/5. Gait: walks with cane assist, slow but steady, no head lag.
Skin: Diffuse fine residual scaling, much improved. Mild residual hyperpigmentation on chest and abdomen. No active rash. Healing biopsy sites.
Neuro: Speech normal volume and resonance; no dysarthria or dysphonia. Cranial nerves intact (palate elevation symmetric). DTRs 2+ symmetric. Sensation intact.
Swallow screen: 5 mL water by spoon — clean swallow, no cough; mother witnesses she ate apple slices in waiting room without difficulty.

ASSESSMENT:

23-year-old woman with active SLE-associated inflammatory myositis (overlap PM/DM features) with severe protein-calorie malnutrition / kwashiorkor, now 4 weeks post-discharge with EXCELLENT EARLY RESPONSE TO COMBINED IMMUNOSUPPRESSION + NUTRITIONAL REHABILITATION:

* Disease activity markers continuing to improve (CK 218 from peak 2580; dsDNA 16 from peak 52; complements normalizing; UPC normalized).
* Functional recovery substantial: walking with cane, full oral diet, NG tube removed.
* Nutritional recovery substantial: 9.4 kg weight gain in 5 weeks, albumin 3.4 (up from 1.6), prealbumin 28 (now normal range), all micronutrients repleted.
* Pancytopenia resolved.
* Renal involvement resolved (UPC < 0.2); deferring biopsy.
* Skin findings markedly improved.
* Mood improving (PHQ-9 6, mild).
* No infectious complications. No medication intolerances.

PLAN:

1. Continue mycophenolate 1.5 g PO BID (maintenance for 12-18 months minimum).
2. Continue prednisone taper: 25 mg next week, then decrease by 5 mg every 2 weeks to 10 mg, then by 2.5 mg every month.
3. Continue hydroxychloroquine 200 mg BID indefinitely.
4. Continue levothyroxine 50 mcg daily.
5. Continue PCP prophylaxis with TMP/SMX until off prednisone for >3 months.
6. Continue calcium + vitamin D + multivitamin. Discontinue specific zinc/selenium repletion (completed courses).
7. Continue PO diet ad lib. Discontinue all enteral feeds. Dietitian to monitor.
8. PT: continue community-based PT 2x/week; goal: independent ambulation without cane in 4-6 weeks.
9. Continue Esperanza Behavioral Health therapy weekly.
10. Follow-up: rheumatology in 6 weeks (6/3/2027) — first PERMANENT-Medicaid-covered visit anticipated (Medicaid approved 4/8/2027! Patient now has Medicaid through 12/31/2027).
11. Continue Esperanza PCP follow-up every 4 weeks.
12. Outpatient labs every 4 weeks for first 6 months: CBC, CMP, ESR, CRP, dsDNA, C3/C4, UPC, CK, TSH.
13. Ophthalmology baseline HCQ exam was completed 4/3 (free clinic) — normal exam, recommended annual screening.
14. Patient will return to part-time work (housekeeping, modified duties) tentatively in 4-6 weeks per OT clearance; OT plans formal work-readiness evaluation 5/15/2027.
15. Contraception counseling — re-engagement when patient is ready.
16. Discharge return precautions reinforced.

Long-term outlook: Maria has had an excellent early response to treatment, recovering from a life-threatening illness with multidisciplinary support. Her long-term prognosis depends on (1) maintaining immunosuppression adherence, which now has insurance support; (2) preventing future flares with vigilance for early symptoms; (3) sustainable nutritional rehabilitation with cultural and family support; and (4) ongoing social-determinant support to prevent the access-mediated decompensations of the prior year. The team is cautiously optimistic that with continued engagement she can return to a functional and meaningful life.

Next visit: 6/3/2027 (6 weeks).

Visit time: 50 minutes (interpreter time 30 minutes).
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Jessica Tanaka, MD
Rheumatology, Johns Hopkins
Date: 4/22/2027
